description: Armor, helmets, shields, and protective equipment worn into battle or daily life.
items: Amulet
A protective charm on a tarnished chain, cool against the chest and faintly humming.
Anklet
A delicate chain circling the ankle, jingling faintly with each stride.
Arcane Focus
A polished crystal rod or carved wand channeling raw magic through a single point.
Armband
A thick coil of metal gripping the upper arm, a warrior's mark of rank.
Assassin's Cloak
A midnight-dark hooded wrap absorbing torchlight, rustling no louder than a drawn breath.
Aventail
A curtain of fine mail hanging from the helmet rim, guarding the neck and jawline.
Backpack
A framed canvas pack riding high on the shoulders, creaking under days of provisions.
Badge
A small enameled pin fastened to the breast, declaring allegiance at a glance.
Baldric
A diagonal shoulder strap crossing the chest, bearing a sword's weight on the opposite hip.
Banded Shield
A round wooden shield reinforced with riveted iron bands, sturdy against slashing but heavy on the arm.
Barbarian Furs
Layered pelts of wolf and bear draped across the shoulders, bristling and rank with musk.
Barbuta
A sleek Italian helm with a T-shaped opening framing the eyes and chin.
Bard's Costume
A slashed-sleeve doublet of mismatched bright silks, impossible to overlook in any crowd.
Bascinet
A pointed steel cap extending down to guard the neck, sharp-lined and angular.
Bedroll
A rolled bundle of blankets and oilcloth, tied with cord and slung across the pack.
Belt
A thick leather strap buckled at the waist, hung with loops for blade and purse.
Besagews
Small circular discs tied at the armpit, plugging the gap where arm meets torso.
Blanket
A rough wool sheet folded tight for travel, smelling of campfire smoke and damp earth.
Bracer
A stiff leather guard strapped to the bow arm, shielding skin from the string's brutal snap.
Bracelet
A coiled band of hammered metal clasped around the wrist, warm from the skin beneath.
Bracers
Curved plates strapped along the forearm, catching blades aimed below the elbow.
Breeches
Knee-length trousers of fitted cloth, worn tucked into tall boots or stockings.
Breastplate
A curved steel plate molded to the chest, turning aside lance points and sword edges.
Brigandine
Small overlapping steel plates riveted inside stiff canvas, flexible yet strong.
Brooch
An ornate clasping pin fastening cloak to collar, jeweled or enameled for display.
Buckler
A fist-sized metal disc gripped in one hand, quick to parry but too small for real cover.
Byrnie
A short mail shirt ending at the waist, lighter and quicker to don than a hauberk.
Cap
A close-fitting cloth or leather head covering, plain and practical against wind and chill.
Cape
A short shoulder-drape falling to the elbows, dramatic but offering little real warmth.
Chain Mail
Thousands of interlocking iron rings forming a shimmering, heavy mesh across the torso.
Chainmail
Interlocked iron rings woven into a flexible shirt, heavier than it looks and noisy on the move.
Charm
A tiny carved figure or knotted cord dangling from a chain, rubbed for luck.
Chausses
Full-length mail leggings laced from hip to ankle, dragging with their own weight.
Chemise
A loose linen undergarment worn against the skin, soft and faintly yellowed with use.
Circlet
A thin ring of silver resting on the brow, elegant and almost weightless.
Cloak
A broad sweep of heavy wool clasped at the throat, billowing behind in wind and rain.
Coat of Plates
Iron rectangles sewn between layers of leather, bulky but reliable protection.
Coif
A hood of interlocking mail draping over head and neck, flexible but noisy.
Collar
A stiff band of leather or metal buckled around the neck, marking servitude or warding off blade strikes.
Combat Boots
Iron-shod leather boots laced tight to the ankle, heavy enough to feel on every step.
Component Pouch
A many-pocketed belt pouch clinking with glass vials, dried herbs, and pinches of powder.
Corinthian Helmet
An ancient bronze shell enclosing the entire head, leaving only eyes and mouth exposed.
Couters
Hinged elbow cups allowing a full bend while guarding the joint from hammer blows.
Cowl
A deep hood shadowing the face to the chin, hiding features in pooled darkness.
Crest
A heraldic emblem stitched into fabric or stamped on plate, tracing bloodline and house.
Crown
A band of gold set with gemstones, heavy with the weight of authority.
Cuirass
Matched front and back plates buckled at the sides, encasing the torso in rigid steel.
Cuisses
Plate armor wrapping the thigh from hip to knee, solid and restrictive.
Diadem
A jeweled band arching across the forehead, lighter than a crown but equally commanding.
Doublet
A close-fitted jacket of fine wool with rows of brass buttons, marking its wearer as gentry.
Dragon Scale Armor
Iridescent overlapping scales pried from a wyrm's hide, warm to the touch and fireproof.
Dress
A single flowing garment from shoulder to ankle, cinched at the waist with cord or sash.
Dwarven Mail
Dense rings of blue-gray steel forged in mountain heat, heavier and finer than human craft.
Earring
A small metal hoop or jeweled stud piercing one lobe, glinting beneath loose hair.
Earrings
Small metal loops or studs piercing the lobes, glinting beneath the hair.
Elbow Pads
Quilted pads laced at each elbow, absorbing scrapes and falls without restricting reach.
Elven Cloak
A gossamer-thin weave shifting color with the forest light, nearly invisible among leaves.
Faulds
Horizontal bands of steel skirting the hips, bridging the gap between breastplate and legs.
Fibula
An ancient arched pin of bronze, both fastener and ornament in one curved piece.
Finger Guard
A fitted leather tab protecting the drawing fingers from the bowstring's bite.
Frog
A stitched leather loop riveted to the belt, cradling a sheathed blade for quick release.
Gambeson
Thick quilted linen stuffed with wool, surprisingly effective at absorbing blunt strikes.
Gauntlets
Articulated steel plates sheathing each finger and knuckle, cold and unyielding.
Gloves
Close-fitting hand coverings of stitched leather, supple enough for gripping a blade.
Goggles
Tinted glass lenses set in padded leather frames, shielding the eyes from glare, dust, and wind.
Gorget
A collar of curved steel plates encircling the throat, cold against the skin beneath.
Gorget Plates
Overlapping articulated throat plates flexing with each turn of the head.
Great Helm
A full-face steel shell with narrow eye slits, muffling sound and limiting vision.
Greaves
Shaped steel plates buckled over the shins, ringing like bells when struck.
Hair Pin
A slender spike of silver or bone holding braids in place, doubling as a hidden tool.
Harness
A fitted arrangement of leather straps and iron buckles, distributing weight across chest and shoulders.
Hat
A broad felt brim shading the eyes, battered by sun and rain into soft shapelessness.
Hauberk
A knee-length shirt of woven mail rings, heavy on the shoulders and slow to dry.
Headband
A strip of cloth or thin leather tied around the brow, keeping hair and sweat from the eyes.
Headdress
An elaborate arrangement of feathers, beads, and dyed cloth crowning the head, ceremonial and striking.
Heater Shield
A flat-topped triangular shield of painted wood, the classic arms of a mounted knight.
Helmet
A rounded steel cap with a chinstrap and shallow brim, simple and serviceable for any soldier.
Holy Symbol
A silver icon hanging from a chain, warm in the hand during whispered prayers.
Horned Helmet
A brutal helm crowned with curving horn projections, more terrifying than practical.
Hose
Close-fitting cloth leggings pulled from toe to hip, thin and easily snagged.
Insignia
An embroidered patch or metal device sewn to the shoulder, marking unit and rank.
Jerkin
A tough sleeveless jacket of plain leather, practical and hard-wearing for laborers.
Kettle Hat
A wide-brimmed iron hat deflecting rain and glancing blows alike.
Kite Shield
A tall teardrop of layered wood and leather, covering shoulder to knee on horseback.
Knee Pads
Padded leather caps tied behind the knee, cushioning kneeling on stone and rough ground.
Lamellar
Narrow lacquered plates laced together in tight horizontal rows, rigid and rattling.
Lantern
A candle set behind panes of thin horn, throwing a weak but steady glow through the dark.
Leather Armor
Hardened rawhide shaped to the body, lightweight but offering only modest defense.
Leather Boots
Calf-high boots of thick cowhide with stiff soles, built for rough ground and long roads.
Leather Gloves
Supple hide gloves fitted close to the hand, offering grip and warmth without bulk.
Leggings
Fitted cloth or soft leather leg coverings laced from ankle to thigh, flexible and warm.
Locket
A tiny hinged case on a chain, clicking open to reveal a painted portrait inside.
Mage Hood
A deep cowl stitched with faintly glowing thread, humming with residual enchantment.
Mantle
A full-shouldered ceremonial cloak lined with fur, heavy with dignity and rank.
Mask
A shaped covering of leather or painted wood concealing the face, unsettling in its stillness.
Medallion
A broad disc of stamped metal resting heavy on the chest, displaying rank or guild.
Mittens
Thick wool coverings keeping fingers together for warmth, clumsy but effective against cold.
Moccasins
Soft deerskin shoes sewn with sinew, silent on forest floors and mossy stone.
Monk's Robes
Loose unbleached linen wraps leaving the limbs free, plain as a vow of poverty.
Morion
A high-crested open helm with a curving brim, favored by pikemen and guards.
Nasal Helmet
A simple iron dome with a broad nose guard casting a shadow across the face.
Necklace
A fine chain resting against the collarbone, catching light with each small movement.
Noble's Doublet
A tailored jacket of velvet and gold thread, stiff with embroidery and self-importance.
Orc Hide Armor
Crudely stitched beast-skin plates lashed with sinew, reeking of smoke and dried blood.
Outfit
A matched set of garments — shirt, trousers, and belt — plain but clean and ready to wear.
Paladin's Surcoat
A white tabard emblazoned with a sunburst crest, worn bright and clean over battle-scarred plate.
Pauldrons
Broad, layered shoulder plates flaring outward, shielding the joint from overhead strikes.
Pavise
A towering rectangular shield propped on the ground, sheltering crossbowmen while they reload.
Peasant's Garb
A patched tunic and rough-spun trousers faded to the color of dry mud.
Pendant
A weighted ornament hanging from a cord against the chest, cool to the touch.
Plackart
An extra plate bolted over the lower breastplate, doubling protection at the belly.
Poleyns
Domed knee guards with side wings, protecting the joint during charges and kneeling.
Pouch
A drawstring pocket of scuffed leather hanging from the belt, lumpy with small odds and ends.
Poulaines
Absurdly long-toed shoes tapering to a point, fashionable but impractical for anything brisk.
Prayer Beads
A string of worn wooden beads rubbed smooth by countless hours of devotion.
Purse
A cinched leather bag for coins, clinking softly against the hip with every step.
Quiver
A stiff leather tube slung across the back, bristling with fletched arrow shafts.
Ranger's Cloak
A mottled green-and-brown wrap blending into underbrush, stained with sap and trail dust.
Rerebraces
Tubular plates encircling the upper arm from shoulder to elbow, clinking with each gesture.
Riding Boots
Tall boots with a pronounced heel catching the stirrup, polished smooth from the saddle.
Ring
A slim band of polished metal circling the finger, sometimes set with a rough-cut stone.
Robe
A long draping garment of dyed cloth, loose-fitting and favored by scholars.
Robes
Flowing layers of dyed linen falling to the ankles, loose enough to conceal whatever lies beneath.
Rope
A coil of hempen cord fraying at the ends, stiff with salt and age.
Sabatons
Articulated metal shoes with overlapping toe plates, clanking heavily on stone floors.
Sabots
Carved wooden clogs hollowed for the foot, clunking loudly on cobblestones.
Sallet
A swept-back visor helmet sitting low over the brow, light enough for long marches.
Sandals
Open-strapped soles of tanned leather, airy and suited for dust roads in warm seasons.
Sash
A wide band of colored silk tied at the waist, marking rank or sworn allegiance.
Satchel
A flap-topped shoulder bag of waxed canvas, stuffed with scrolls and traveling necessities.
Scale Mail
Hundreds of thumb-sized metal scales layered like fish skin over a cloth backing.
Scabbard
A wood-and-leather sheath belted at the hip, its throat worn smooth from countless draws.
Scarf
A long strip of dyed wool wound around the throat, warm and trailing in the breeze.
Schynbalds
Simple shin plates strapped over cloth or leather, basic and quick to fasten.
Seal
A heavy signet pressed into soft wax, leaving a crisp impression of authority.
Shawl
A wide rectangle of woven cloth draped across the shoulders, light and easily shed.
Shield
A broad wooden disc faced with iron, battered and splintered from blocking heavy blows.
Shin Guards
Light plates of hardened leather strapped over the shins, simple and quick to buckle on.
Shirt
A simple pullover of woven linen or cotton, thin and breathable against the skin.
Shoes
Simple stitched leather covering the foot, worn thin at the toe from daily use.
Sigil
A pendant inscribed with arcane symbols, faintly pulsing with bound power.
Signet Ring
A heavy ring bearing an engraved crest, pressed into hot wax to seal letters.
Skirt
A gathered panel of fabric hanging from the waist, swinging freely with movement.
Skull Cap
A plain bowl of hammered steel covering only the crown, hidden easily under a hood.
Sleeve
A detachable tube of cloth or mail laced to the shoulder, protecting the arm independently.
Slippers
Thin cloth shoes with no sole to speak of, meant only for warm floors indoors.
Soft Boots
Thin-soled leather wraps hugging the foot, quiet as bare skin on stone.
Spangenhelm
Riveted metal bands and fitted plates forming a segmented dome over the skull.
Spaulders
Compact shoulder caps sitting close to the body, lighter and less imposing than pauldrons.
Spell Component
A pinch of rare dust or a dried root, consumed in a flash when the incantation is spoken.
Spur
A pointed metal prong buckled to the heel, urging a mount forward with the lightest press.
Spurs
Pointed metal wheels buckled to the heels, jangling with each step on the ground.
Standard
A standing mail collar worn without a helm, protecting the throat on its own.
Stirrups
Iron loops dangling from the saddle, bracing the rider's feet for balance and leverage.
Studded Armor
Boiled leather reinforced with rows of iron studs, stiff and creaking but sturdier than plain hide.
Studded Leather
Boiled hide punched through with iron rivets, stiff and creaking with every movement.
Surcoat
A sleeveless overshirt draped across armor, blazoned with colors that mark allegiance.
Tabard
A loose sleeveless overgarment blazoned with heraldry, worn proudly over armor.
Talisman
A carved bone or etched stone bound in cord, believed to ward off ill fortune.
Targe
A small round shield with a spiked boss, gripped close and used as much for punching.
Tassets
Hanging plates suspended from the waist, swaying with each step to shield the upper thighs.
Thieves' Gloves
Fingerless black leather gloves worn butter-soft, letting nimble fingers work locks unhindered.
Thumb Ring
A smooth bone or jade ring worn on the thumb, easing the draw and release of arrows.
Token
A small keepsake worn close to the skin, scratched and faded but fiercely cherished.
Torc
A rigid open ring of twisted metal pressing against the throat, cold and unyielding.
Torch
A pitch-soaked rag wrapped on a stick, guttering and spitting orange light.
Trousers
Sturdy cloth legs tied or belted at the waist, practical and universal for travelers.
Tunic
A simple pull-over shirt of rough linen, the universal garment of the common folk.
Vambraces
Lightweight forearm guards of hardened leather, snug enough for drawing a bowstring.
Vest
A snug sleeveless layer worn over a shirt, adding warmth without hindering the arms.
Wig
A fitted cap of woven hair — human or horsehair — styled and powdered to conceal what lies beneath.
Wizard Hat
A tall cone of stiffened felt tapering to a bent point, unmistakable from any distance.
Wrist Guards
Stiff leather bands wrapped tight around each wrist, bracing the joint against jarring impacts.
